fault_hypothesis: Line 78 uses a simple `int(v)` (floor) conversion which loses fractional remainders when weights don't divide evenly, so the sum of integer allocations is less than `total`. The fix requires a remainder-distributing rounding (e.g. largest-remainder method) instead of plain floor.

spec.expr: sum(int(v) for v in shares__1) == int(total)

constraints.intent: After flooring each share, the sum of integer allocations must equal total; this is violated when weights cause fractional remainders.
The raw shares must sum to total before any rounding is applied.
The returned integer list must sum exactly to total, catching the floor-rounding deficit.
The number of computed shares must match the number of weight slots before use.
